NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. The king shall have joy in Your strength, O LORD; And in Your salvation how greatly shall he rejoice!
NKJV.2: You have given him his heart’s desire, And have not withheld the request of his lips. Selah
NKJV.3: For You meet him with the blessings of goodness; You set a crown of pure gold upon his head.
NKJV.4: He asked life from You, and You gave it to him— Length of days forever and ever.
NKJV.5: His glory is great in Your salvation; Honor and majesty You have placed upon him.
NKJV.6: For You have made him most blessed forever;  You have made him exceedingly glad with Your presence.
NKJV.7: For the king trusts in the LORD, And through the mercy of the Most High he shall not be moved.
NKJV.8: Your hand will find all Your enemies; Your right hand will find those who hate You.
NKJV.9: You shall make them as a fiery oven in the time of Your anger; The LORD shall swallow them up in His wrath, And the fire shall devour them.
NKJV.10: Their offspring You shall destroy from the earth, And their descendants from among the sons of men.
NKJV.11: For they intended evil against You; They devised a plot which they are not able to perform.
NKJV.12: Therefore You will make them turn their back; You will make ready Your arrows on Your string toward their faces.
NKJV.13: Be exalted, O LORD, in Your own strength! We will sing and praise Your power.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. The king<S>4428</S> shall joy<S>8055</S> in thy strength<S>5797</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>; and in thy salvation<S>3444</S> how greatly<S>3966</S> shall he rejoice<S>1523</S>!
KJV.2: Thou hast given<S>5414</S> him his heart's<S>3820</S> desire<S>8378</S>, and hast not withholden<S>4513</S> the request<S>782</S> of his lips<S>8193</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.3: For thou preventest<S>6923</S> him with the blessings<S>1293</S> of goodness<S>2896</S>: thou settest<S>7896</S> a crown<S>5850</S> of pure gold<S>6337</S> on his head<S>7218</S>.
KJV.4: He asked<S>7592</S> life<S>2416</S> of thee, and thou gavest<S>5414</S> it him, even length<S>753</S> of days<S>3117</S> for ever<S>5769</S> and ever<S>5703</S>.
KJV.5: His glory<S>3519</S> is great<S>1419</S> in thy salvation<S>3444</S>: honour<S>1935</S> and majesty<S>1926</S> hast thou laid<S>7737</S> upon him.
KJV.6: For thou hast made<S>7896</S> him most blessed<S>1293</S> for ever<S>5703</S>: thou hast made<S>2302</S> him exceeding glad<S>8057</S> with thy countenance<S>6440</S>. <sup>made him most: Heb. set him to be blessings</sup> <sup>made him exceeding: Heb. make him glad with joy</sup>
KJV.7: For the king<S>4428</S> trusteth<S>982</S> in the LORD<S>3068</S>, and through the mercy<S>2617</S> of the most High<S>5945</S> he shall not be moved<S>4131</S>.
KJV.8: Thine hand<S>3027</S> shall find out<S>4672</S> all thine enemies<S>341</S>: thy right hand<S>3225</S> shall find out<S>4672</S> those that hate<S>8130</S> thee.
KJV.9: Thou shalt make<S>7896</S> them as a fiery<S>784</S> oven<S>8574</S> in the time<S>6256</S> of thine anger<S>6440</S>: the LORD<S>3068</S> shall swallow them up<S>1104</S> in his wrath<S>639</S>, and the fire<S>784</S> shall devour<S>398</S> them.
KJV.10: Their fruit<S>6529</S> shalt thou destroy<S>6</S> from the earth<S>776</S>, and their seed<S>2233</S> from among the children<S>1121</S> of men<S>120</S>.
KJV.11: For they intended<S>5186</S> evil<S>7451</S> against thee: they imagined<S>2803</S> a mischievous device<S>4209</S>, which they are not able<S>3201</S> to perform.
KJV.12: Therefore shalt thou make<S>7896</S> them turn their back<S>7926</S>, when thou shalt make ready<S>3559</S> thine arrows upon thy strings<S>4340</S> against the face<S>6440</S> of them. <sup>shalt thou: or, thou shalt set them as a butt</sup> <sup>back: Heb. shoulder</sup>
KJV.13: Be thou exalted<S>7311</S>, LORD<S>3068</S>, in thine own strength<S>5797</S>: so will we sing<S>7891</S> and praise<S>2167</S> thy power<S>1369</S>.

ESV.1: O LORD, in your strength the king rejoices, and in your salvation how greatly he exults!
ESV.2: You have given him his heart's desire and have not withheld the request of his lips. Selah
ESV.3: For you meet him with rich blessings; you set a crown of fine gold upon his head.
ESV.4: He asked life of you; you gave it to him, length of days forever and ever.
ESV.5: His glory is great through your salvation; splendor and majesty you bestow on him.
ESV.6: For you make him most blessed forever;  you make him glad with the joy of your presence.
ESV.7: For the king trusts in the LORD, and through the steadfast love of the Most High he shall not be moved.
ESV.8: Your hand will find out all your enemies; your right hand will find out those who hate you.
ESV.9: You will make them as a blazing oven when you appear. The LORD will swallow them up in his wrath, and fire will consume them.
ESV.10: You will destroy their descendants from the earth, and their offspring from among the children of man.
ESV.11: Though they plan evil against you, though they devise mischief, they will not succeed.
ESV.12: For you will put them to flight; you will aim at their faces with your bows.
ESV.13: Be exalted, O LORD, in your strength! We will sing and praise your power.

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Psalm of David. O LORD, in Your strength the king will be glad, And in Your salvation how greatly he will rejoice!
NASB.2: You have given him his heart's desire, And You have not withheld the request of his lips. Selah.
NASB.3: For You meet him with the blessings of good things; You set a crown of fine gold on his head.
NASB.4: He asked life of You, You gave it to him, Length of days forever and ever.
NASB.5: His glory is great through Your salvation, Splendor and majesty You place upon him.
NASB.6: For You make him most blessed forever; You make him joyful with gladness in Your presence.
NASB.7: For the king trusts in the LORD, And through the lovingkindness of the Most High he will not be shaken.
NASB.8: Your hand will find out all your enemies; Your right hand will find out those who hate you.
NASB.9: You will make them as a fiery oven in the time of your anger; The LORD will swallow them up in His wrath, And fire will devour them.
NASB.10: Their offspring You will destroy from the earth, And their descendants from among the sons of men.
NASB.11: Though they intended evil against You [And] devised a plot, They will not succeed.
NASB.12: For You will make them turn their back; You will aim with Your bowstrings at their faces.
NASB.13: Be exalted, O LORD, in Your strength; We will sing and praise Your power.

NLT.1:
How the king rejoices in your strength, O LORD!
He shouts with joy because you give him victory.
NLT.2:
For you have given him his heart’s desire;
you have withheld nothing he requested. Interlude
NLT.3:
You welcomed him back with success and prosperity.
You placed a crown of finest gold on his head.
NLT.4:
He asked you to preserve his life,
and you granted his request.
The days of his life stretch on forever.
NLT.5:
Your victory brings him great honor,
and you have clothed him with splendor and majesty.
NLT.6:
You have endowed him with eternal blessings
and given him the joy of your presence.
NLT.7:
For the king trusts in the LORD.
The unfailing love of the Most High will keep him from stumbling.
NLT.8:
You will capture all your enemies.
Your strong right hand will seize all who hate you.
NLT.9:
You will throw them in a flaming furnace
when you appear.
The LORD will consume them in his anger;
fire will devour them.
NLT.10:
You will wipe their children from the face of the earth;
they will never have descendants.
NLT.11:
Although they plot against you,
their evil schemes will never succeed.
NLT.12:
For they will turn and run
when they see your arrows aimed at them.
NLT.13:
Rise up, O LORD, in all your power.
With music and singing we celebrate your mighty acts.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. The king rejoices in your strength, Yahweh! How greatly he rejoices in your salvation!
WEB.2: You have given him his heart’s desire, and have not withheld the request of his lips. Selah.
WEB.3: For you meet him with the blessings of goodness. You set a crown of fine gold on his head.
WEB.4: He asked life of you, you gave it to him, even length of days forever and ever.
WEB.5: His glory is great in your salvation. You lay honor and majesty on him.
WEB.6: For you make him most blessed forever. You make him glad with joy in your presence.
WEB.7: For the king trusts in Yahweh. Through the loving kindness of the Most High, he shall not be moved.
WEB.8: Your hand will find out all of your enemies. Your right hand will find out those who hate you.
WEB.9: You will make them as a fiery furnace in the time of your anger. Yahweh will swallow them up in his wrath. The fire shall devour them.
WEB.10: You will destroy their descendants from the earth, their posterity from among the children of men.
WEB.11: For they intended evil against you. They plotted evil against you which cannot succeed.
WEB.12: For you will make them turn their back, when you aim drawn bows at their face.
WEB.13: Be exalted, Yahweh, in your strength, so we will sing and praise your power.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director. A psalm of David. [1] LORD, the king finds joy in your strength.
How greatly he rejoices in your victory! ⓕ
CSB17.2:
You have given him his heart’s desire ⓖ
and have not denied the request of his lips. ⓗ Selah
CSB17.3:
For you meet him with rich blessings;
you place a crown of pure gold on his head. ⓘ
CSB17.4:
He asked you for life, and you gave it to him —
length of days forever and ever. ⓙ
CSB17.5:
His glory is great through your victory;
you confer majesty and splendor on him. ⓚ
CSB17.6:
You give him blessings forever; ⓛ
you cheer him with joy in your presence. ⓜ
CSB17.7:
For the king relies on the LORD;
through the faithful love of the Most High
he is not shaken. ⓝ
CSB17.8:
Your hand will capture all your enemies;
your right hand will seize those who hate you. ⓐ
CSB17.9:
You will make them burn
like a fiery furnace when you appear;
the LORD will engulf them in his wrath,
and fire will devour them. ⓑ
CSB17.10:
You will wipe their progeny from the earth
and their offspring from the human race. [1] ⓒ
CSB17.11:
Though they intend to harm [2] you
and devise a wicked plan, they will not prevail. ⓓ
CSB17.12:
Instead, you will put them to flight
when you ready your bowstrings to shoot at them. ⓔ
CSB17.13:
Be exalted, LORD, in your strength;
we will sing and praise your might. ⓕ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. O LORD the king will delight in Your strength,
And in Your salvation how greatly will he rejoice!
AMP.2:
You have given him his heart's desire,
And You have not withheld the request of his lips. Selah.
AMP.3:
For You meet him with blessings of good things;
You set a crown of pure gold on his head.
AMP.4:
He asked life of You,
And You gave it to him,
Long life forever and evermore.
AMP.5:
His glory is great because of Your victory;
Splendor and majesty You bestow upon him.
AMP.6:
For You make him most blessed [and a blessing] forever;
You make him joyful with the joy of Your presence.
AMP.7:
For the king [confidently] trusts in the LORD,
And through the lovingkindness (faithfulness, goodness) of the Most High he will never be shaken.
AMP.8:
Your hand will reach out and defeat all your enemies;
Your right hand will reach those who hate you.
AMP.9:
You will make them as [if in] a blazing oven in the time of your anger;
The LORD will swallow them up in His wrath,
And the fire will devour them.
AMP.10:
Their offspring You will destroy from the earth,
And their descendants from the sons of men.
AMP.11:
For they planned evil against You;
They devised a [malevolent] plot
And they will not succeed.
AMP.12:
For You will make them turn their backs [in defeat];
You will aim Your bowstring [of divine justice] at their faces.
AMP.13:
Be exalted, LORD, in Your strength;
We will sing and praise Your power.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Your strength, God, is the king's strength.
Helped, he's hollering Hosannas.
MSG.2:
You gave him exactly what he wanted;
you didn't hold back.
MSG.3:
You filled his arms with gifts;
you gave him a right royal welcome.
MSG.4:
He wanted a good life; you gave it to him,
and then made it a long life as a bonus.
MSG.5:
You lifted him high and bright as a cumulus cloud,
then dressed him in rainbow colors.
MSG.6:
You pile blessings on him;
you make him glad when you smile.
MSG.7:
Is it any wonder the king loves God?
that he's sticking with the Best?
MSG.8:
With a fistful of enemies in one hand
and a fistful of haters in the other,
MSG.9:
You radiate with such brilliance
that they cringe as before a furnace.
Now the furnace swallows them whole,
the fire eats them alive!
MSG.10:
You purge the earth of their progeny,
you wipe the slate clean.
MSG.11:
All their evil schemes, the plots they cook up,
have fizzled—every one.
MSG.12:
You sent them packing;
they couldn't face you.
MSG.13:
Show your strength, God, so no one can miss it.
We are out singing the good news!

NIV.1:
Psalm 21
For the director of music. A psalm of David.
O Lord, the king rejoices in your strength.
How great is his joy in the victories you give!
NIV.2:
You have granted him the desire of his heart
and have not withheld the request of his lips. Selah
NIV.3:
You welcomed him with rich blessings
and placed a crown of pure gold on his head.
NIV.4:
He asked you for life, and you gave it to him—
length of days, for ever and ever.
NIV.5:
Through the victories you gave, his glory is great;
you have bestowed on him splendor and majesty.
NIV.6:
Surely you have granted him eternal blessings
and made him glad with the joy of your presence.
NIV.7:
For the king trusts in the Lord;
through the unfailing love of the Most High
he will not be shaken.
NIV.8:
Your hand will lay hold on all your enemies;
your right hand will seize your foes.
NIV.9:
At the time of your appearing
you will make them like a fiery furnace.
In his wrath the Lord will swallow them up,
and his fire will consume them.
NIV.10:
You will destroy their descendants from the earth,
their posterity from mankind.
NIV.11:
Though they plot evil against you
and devise wicked schemes, they cannot succeed;
NIV.12:
for you will make them turn their backs
when you aim at them with drawn bow.
NIV.13:
Be exalted, O Lord, in your strength;
we will sing and praise your might.

NRSVCE.1:
In your strength the king rejoices, O LORD,
and in your help how greatly he exults!
NRSVCE.2:
You have given him his heart’s desire,
and have not withheld the request of his lips. Selah
NRSVCE.3:
For you meet him with rich blessings;
you set a crown of fine gold on his head.
NRSVCE.4:
He asked you for life; you gave it to him —
length of days forever and ever.
NRSVCE.5:
His glory is great through your help;
splendor and majesty you bestow on him.
NRSVCE.6:
You bestow on him blessings forever;
you make him glad with the joy of your presence.
NRSVCE.7:
For the king trusts in the LORD,
and through the steadfast love of the Most High he shall not be moved.
NRSVCE.8:
Your hand will find out all your enemies;
your right hand will find out those who hate you.
NRSVCE.9:
You will make them like a fiery furnace
when you appear.
The LORD will swallow them up in his wrath,
and fire will consume them.
NRSVCE.10:
You will destroy their offspring from the earth,
and their children from among humankind.
NRSVCE.11:
If they plan evil against you,
if they devise mischief, they will not succeed.
NRSVCE.12:
For you will put them to flight;
you will aim at their faces with your bows.
NRSVCE.13:
Be exalted, O LORD, in your strength!
We will sing and praise your power.

NET.1: O LORD, the king rejoices in the strength you give; he takes great delight in the deliverance you provide.
NET.2: You grant him his heart’s desire; you do not refuse his request. (Selah)
NET.3: For you bring him rich blessings; you place a golden crown on his head.
NET.4: He asked you to sustain his life, and you have granted him long life and an enduring dynasty.
NET.5: Your deliverance brings him great honor; you give him majestic splendor.
NET.6: For you grant him lasting blessings; you give him great joy by allowing him into your presence.
NET.7: For the king trusts in the LORD, and because of the sovereign LORD’s faithfulness he is not upended.
NET.8: You prevail over all your enemies; your power is too great for those who hate you.
NET.9: You burn them up like a fiery furnace when you appear; the LORD angrily devours them; the fire consumes them.
NET.10: You destroy their offspring from the earth, their descendants from among the human race.
NET.11: Yes, they intend to do you harm; they dream up a scheme, but they do not succeed.
NET.12: For you make them retreat when you shoot your arrows at them.
NET.13: Rise up, O LORD, in strength! We will sing and praise your power!
